events.title: US Strikes Iranian Tankers to Enforce Blockade
events.desc: US forces, utilizing an F/A-18 Super Hornet, fired precision munitions at two Iranian-flagged tankers, disabling them to prevent them from violating the American blockade of Iran's ports. This action follows a previous incident where a third Iranian-flagged vessel was disabled. US Central Command confirmed the strikes, emphasizing that the vessels are no longer transiting to Iran.

events.title: UAE Air Defenses Engage Missile and Drone Threats from Iran
events.desc: The United Arab Emirates reported that its air defense systems were actively engaging missile and drone threats originating from Iran. This incident occurs amid ongoing tensions and a shaky ceasefire between the US and Iran, further testing the fragile peace.

events.title: Israel Strikes Hezbollah Target in Beirut
events.desc: The Israeli military conducted a strike in Beirut, targeting a senior Hezbollah figure. This marks the first strike of its kind in the capital since a mid-April ceasefire, signaling a potential escalation of hostilities despite previous de-escalation efforts.

events.title: Iran Accuses US of 'Reckless Military Adventure'
events.desc: Iran's Abbas Araghchi condemned US actions, labeling them a 'reckless military adventure.' He stated that the US escalates its attacks precisely when diplomatic solutions appear to be within reach, suggesting a pattern of disruption to peace efforts.

events.title: South Korea Investigates Ship Fire in Strait of Hormuz Amid Iran Dispute
events.desc: South Korea's Oceans Ministry has launched an investigation into the cause of an explosion and fire aboard a Korean-operated vessel in the Strait of Hormuz. The incident occurred amid ongoing uncertainty about whether the vessel was attacked during the heightened tensions with Iran.

events.title: US Strikes Iranian-Flagged Tankers Violating Blockade
events.desc: US forces engaged and disabled two Iranian-flagged tankers that attempted to violate an American blockade of Iran's ports. The strikes were carried out by a US Navy F/A-18 Super Hornet, which fired precision munitions into the vessels' smokestacks, preventing their entry into Iran.

events.title: Two Waves of Bombing Revealed at Iran School Strike
events.desc: New verified videos confirm two distinct waves of missile strikes hit an IRGC compound and an adjacent school in Minab, Iran, in late February. The videos, released by Iran's Ministry of Foreign Affairs, show multiple impacts on the complex, providing further detail on the devastating attack.

events.title: US Pauses 'Project Freedom' Operation in Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: US President Trump announced a pause to 'Project Freedom,' an operation to guide vessels through the Strait of Hormuz. The pause comes after progress was reportedly made toward a potential deal with Iran, though the operation had only been active for a short period.

events.title: Iran's Police Infrastructure Targeted in Attacks
events.desc: Bellingcat has documented damage to at least 80 police stations and Basij paramilitary facilities within the first three weeks of the US-Israeli war against Iran. Experts suggest the aim is to degrade the Iranian regime's repressive capacity.

events.title: UAE Downplays Iranian Strikes, Rewrites Narrative
events.desc: Authorities in the UAE have reportedly downplayed the impact and extent of Iranian drone strikes, sometimes mischaracterizing interceptions or failing to acknowledge successful attacks. This divergence between official statements and open-source evidence suggests a deliberate effort to control the narrative surrounding the conflict.

events.title: US Jets Disabled Iranian Tankers in Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: US jets fired on and disabled two Iranian-flagged tankers attempting to violate the American blockade. The strikes, confirmed by US Central Command, were intended to prevent the non-compliant vessels from entering Iranian ports.

events.title: Iran Considering US Proposal Amidst Ongoing Conflict
events.desc: Pakistan, acting as a mediator, stated that Iran is considering a US proposal aimed at ending the ongoing conflict. The mediator's efforts focus on converting the current ceasefire into a permanent cessation of hostilities.

events.title: Pope Leo Calls for Global Leaders to Calm Tensions
events.desc: Following a meeting with US Secretary of State Marco Rubio, Pope Leo appealed for divine inspiration for world leaders to de-escalate global tensions and reduce hatred. The Pope, who has previously criticized the Iran war, urged prayers for governments to turn away from violence.

events.title: Rubio Awaits Iran's Response to US Deal Proposal
events.desc: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio indicated that Washington is expecting Iran's response to US proposals for a deal to end the conflict. He expressed hope for a serious offer that could lead to meaningful negotiation processes, while also criticizing Iran's control over the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: China Calls for Strait of Hormuz Reopening Amidst Iran Talks
events.desc: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi held talks with Iran's Foreign Minister Aragchi, urging for the Strait of Hormuz to be reopened 'as soon as possible.' This discussion occurs as Aragchi makes his first trip to China since the war began, highlighting diplomatic efforts to ease regional tensions.
